fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 14-year-old female brought by mother after expressing wish to 'not wake up' following epilepsy diagnosis.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 14-year-old female diagnosed with epilepsy last week after two seizures. She told her mother this morning she wishes she would not wake up because she cannot imagine living with epilepsy. She endorses passive thoughts of not wanting to be alive but denies plan, method, or intent. She describes fear of having seizures in public, worry about driving restrictions, and feeling 'broken.' Sleep normal, appetite decreased. Just started levetiracetam 500mg BID. Has not told friends about diagnosis. Still attending school but anxious. Plays clarinet in band. Supportive mother and younger brother. No prior mental health concerns. (Pediatric Neurologist Dr. Amanda Ross, MD)

Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Passive suicidal ideation without plan or intent; health anxiety

fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Epilepsy, recently diagnosed; adjustment disorder with anxiety and depressed mood; passive suicidal ideation.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: New epilepsy diagnosis (1 week); two recent seizures; started on antiepileptic medication; has not disclosed to friends; continues band; supportive family.
Assessment_and_Plan: Low risk: passive SI without plan or intent in context of new chronic illness diagnosis.
Protective factors: family support, band involvement, good insight.
Normal adjustment reaction to epilepsy diagnosis.
Counseling for chronic illness adjustment and epilepsy-specific concerns.
Connect with Epilepsy Foundation and teen epilepsy support groups.
Education about epilepsy management and life with well-controlled epilepsy.
Monitor for mood effects of levetiracetam (known side effect).
Encourage disclosure to close friends for safety.
Follow-up with neurology and therapist within one week.
Safe for discharge with appropriate chronic illness support.
fields.Author: Dr. Amanda Ross, MD (Pediatric Neurologist)
